title: werauhia kathyae
intro: Werauhia kathyae, also known as a species of fern in the family Asteraceae, is a rare and endangered plant native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial abilities, which can be used to treat various health conditions. Werauhia kathyae has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is typically harvested, dried, and then used in traditional medicine or as a component in various supplements.
